raw_input:
[5.0/5] All around, a good headset Used this on a PS4 Pro and it worked great- clear sound from the game and in chatting. It's bulky but not heavy and comfortable to wear- even for hours at a time. The mic is a good length and able to bend a bit, if needed. The cord seems durable and connected well to the PS4 controller. The controls for the headset itself all work good. All around, a good headset 👍 [5.0/5] Lot of bang for your buck The quality of the sound is more than the price paid. These headphones will give ones that I paid 10 times the price a run for their money. The cord is plenty long for gaming I just used them on ps4 along with virtual gear and the experience was awesome. I love the way they fit and can't hear much else if anything when wearing this product. One thing I love is the mute Microphone option on them it comes in handy. You won't go regret this purchase and the can't beat the price.

Note
My reviews are honest no matter the product nor the price I paid . If my review helped you in your purchase take a moment and click helpful. [5.0/5] My sons favorite headphones. These are my son’s go to gaming headphones. He loves them. Says they are comfortable, durable, and good sound. Recommended. [5.0/5] Comfortable, responsive, adjustable - overall great! Noise Cancellation:
4/5 - not the best that noise cancelling technology has to offer, but still effective and satisfiable

Comfort:
5/5 - Soft ear pads that don't cause strain even after hours of wearing. Extendable/flexible head band fits all head sizes/shapes without being too tight/loose.

Sound Quality
5/5 - Keeping expectations realistic the sound quality is excellent with plenty of bass/treble, although there is no way to adjust this unless you do so from your audio source. The microphone is responsive and the cable has an adjustable volume dial.

Other:
Cable length is very long (6.6 ft) making it easy to play video games from the couch and relatively light so as to night create neck strain after several hours of use.

Overall:
An excellent headset for gaming, listening to music, skyping, etc. [4.0/5] Good value for the price I am not a serious gamer. I don’t stream or use the microphone much. I mostly wanted these for the headphones for sound.

I think they are great for what I needed. I’m not sure if I would recommend them for serious gamers/streamers.

The cups are very big on the ears, but it feels very comfortable. You can control volume on the cord, which is nice.

I feel like they are a good value for the price. [3.0/5] Comfortable, but loose fitting These headphones have really decent quality for some budget headphones. The sound is pretty clear coming through and the ear pieces are super cushy.

Unfortunately, where these headphones fall short for me is the fit. They don’t fit snug at all, it’s almost as if the ear pieces just hang off the top of my ears. It’s definitely usable, but loose fitting in my opinion.

Other than that, they are pretty monster headphones, so just be sure you’re ready to be wearing size huge. I honestly wouldn’t have a problem with the size of the headphones if they fit better. I’m too afraid to make sudden movements because they feel like they will slip off. [5.0/5] Gaming headphones Got them at a great price my boys love them [4.0/5] My son likes this headset I gave this headset to my 11 year old son. below is his review. From myself I can add that the manual translation is very lazy and disrespectful to the customers.

"I got the headseat and was very eager to use it. The headset definitely did not disappoint. The noise blocking was nice, and the headset felt very comfortable on my ears. For me the headset doesn't fit perfectly, there is a small space between my head and the bottom of the ear pads. I get uses to it after a while but i wish ear pads would fit all the way around. Other than that, I really like the headset and definitely would recommend." [3.0/5] Comfortable Casual Gaming Headset Audio is basic stereo, and nothing to write home about. At best, similar to stock headphones you get with your phone or mp3 player.

Comfort on the the other hand is another story.

Love the padding on ear cups and head rest. Can wear for extended amount of time without any discomfort.

Mic quality is basic. Similar to mic audio on smartphones.

In line controls are huge (for some reason). Has volume slider and mute (on/off) switch. Has enough raised texture/grooves that you're able to blind use the sliders

Works with any device with a 3.5mm headphone jack. Includes a splitter for PC users. Main wire is not detachable from headset

Overall, one of the most comfortable headsets i've tried. Too bad the audio sucks [5.0/5] Excellent Budget Headphones While this gaming headset by Chamvict is certainly not the best sounding headset I have ever used, it is a very good budget headset. The sound quality is basic stereo; no surround sound enhancements here. However, it is clear and crisp stereo sound. The mike came through loud and clear using the windows microphone set up and by way of feedback from fellow gamers. The ear cups are the over the ear type and completely cover my ears blocking out outside noise. The ear cups are soft and well padded. The headband and ear cups are among the most comfortable I have ever used. I gave them to my son to use over the weekend. He played BFV and Death By Daylight with his buddies all weekend. He claims they are more comfortable and much better sounding than his Plantronics. He was using the Plantronics over his wireless Corsair set. Chamvict even includes a Y connector for those that need it. For the current price of $27.99, these budget headphones are 5 Star in my book. If you can get them on sale, they are even a better deal. [4.0/5] Great Value Headphones are so expensive now a days, so these are at a great price point. They work perfect for gaming, especially if you are doing online gaming as they do have a microphone. They do not have too many features, but our a nice pair if you are looking for some. [2.0/5] Not overly impressed They appear to be made well, but the fit isn't great... I could not get them to stay in place on my head and the earphones don't seal well around the ear. Sound was loud enough, but not very clear. The mic seems to work ok. Everyone could hear me just fine. [4.0/5] Better Than Expected When I opened the packaging and examined the hardware of these headphones, I thought I would hate it. The head support on top (the black band) has too much play and I didn't expect they would hold well. The set is very large and heavy lending to my belief. However, when I actually used them I was pleasantly surprised. I was able to wear these for three hours without any slippage issues. The loose band actually seemed to help with comfort, and I found that I didn't have many of the long term use issues I have with some other units (strain, discomfort, things like that).

Sound quality is equal to or better than some others I've tried. I have hearing loss, so my impression of sound quality may not meet what others look for. For me, I need sound that isn't tinny or bassy in the extremes. This unit worked well for me, and I was able to listen to favorite music without any issues.

Noise cancelling was very good and I was able to lock out the outside world pretty easily. The huge size of the headphones helps this by completely enveloping your ears (and maybe your entire head if small enough).

I haven't had an opportunity to try the microphone but I found this to be the most annoying aspect of this unit. The mic is flexible but doesn't stay in a desired position. It wants to always return to the default location which gets in the way. I'm considering adding a piece of velcro to allow me to stick it to the ear piece so the mic ins't stuck right on my face.

For me, I like it so much I've replaced my previous headset at work with this one. I mainly use it for music and conference calls (that will be another test when I actually need to use the mic).

In a landscape where headsets range from price from cheap to absurd and quality doesn't always sync with price I found this one to be a great find. If you don't mind the size of the headset, this is a great value for the price. [4.0/5] Great Value Headphones are so expensive now a days, so these are at a great price point. They work perfect for gaming, especially if you are doing online gaming as they do have a microphone. They do not have too many features, but our a nice pair if you are looking for some. [4.0/5] Great Value Headphones are so expensive now a days, so these are at a great price point. They work perfect for gaming, especially if you are doing online gaming as they do have a microphone. They do not have too many features, but our a nice pair if you are looking for some.

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

Overall, customers are very satisfied with these gaming headphones, with most giving them 4 or 5 stars. The most commonly praised features are comfort, sound quality, and value for the price. Many reviewers note that the soft ear pads and adjustable headband make them comfortable to wear for extended periods. Sound quality is described as clear and crisp, with good bass and treble, and the microphone is generally considered responsive and functional.

Several reviewers specifically mention using them with PS4 and other gaming setups, with positive results. The cord is noted as being a good length for gaming, and the inline volume and mute controls are appreciated. Multiple reviewers say these headphones outperform others they have tried that cost significantly more.

On the negative side, a few reviewers mention that the headphones are bulky and heavy, and some feel they fit loosely and may slip during sudden movements. One reviewer notes that the microphone tends to return to its default position rather than staying where it is placed. A couple of reviewers found the audio quality to be only average, comparable to standard smartphone headphones.

Overall, the consensus is that these are an excellent budget option for casual and serious gamers alike, offering strong value for the price.
